description: Train a top-k sparse autoencoder on the hub residual (L27, 4x expansion, k=24) pre/post-FT, find sub-product-selective features, and causally ablate a single feature at the = position. Driver: modal_app.py sae_suite / sae_feature_ablation.
motivation: Look for a value-specific (product-keyed) feature - the signature of lookup rather than rule - and test whether it is causally used.
formal_results: Best sub-product SAE feature: #5157 |r|=0.558 pre-FT, strengthened to #13802 |r|=0.702 post-FT. Ablating #13802 at L27 = on the 9/48 expressions where it fires drops mean P(BODMAS) 0.994 -> 0.730 (Delta=-0.264), ~0 elsewhere. Caveat: SAEs undertrained (~9.5k/11k dead latents); n=9, no CI.
results_description: A value-specific (b*c-selective) feature exists and is causally used where it fires - consistent with product-keyed lookup, inconsistent with a context-independent rule.
conclusions: Direct evidence of a product-keyed lookup feature that fine-tuning strengthens; the multiply is at least partly memorized lookup, not a clean rule.
